title: Send David package — Houston ask, ACP Xfinity rewards, fixed-fee electricity musing
html:
TL;DR

  • Late-July 2022 to-do covering CS 161B homework, contacting noah dear about midterm/final, and asking david + d3 (likely "bubs and d3") about Houston.
  • Buying ray-bans, Nyjah Huston shoes, calculus-midterm work, and a Seattle bus/train ticket — sophomore-summer logistics.
  • Two signal-rich asides: (a) "Xfinity rewards center.com, call phone number get set up. ACP" — the Affordable Connectivity Program lookup; (b) "can electricity be a fixed fee? Based on historical usage" — a one-line utility-billing-as-business musing that prefigures the later business idea junk drawer notes.
  • The Houston question via bubs + d3 is one of the earlier traces of Yahir considering Texas — five years before the Austin move.
  • Jeff cameo with a 4-digit-PIN coordination — likely a sublet/keys handoff, identity unclear.

Key musings

"can electricity be a fixed fee? Based on historical usage"

"ACP" (Affordable Connectivity Program — internet subsidy he was navigating)

Why it matters

The fixed-fee electricity line is small but on-brand: Yahir keeps noticing pricing structures (variable bills with predictable shapes → why isn't there a flat-fee abstraction?). Same instinct shows up in his later restaurant menu scraping and bromenu thinking — the menu / the bill / the rate-card is a product surface.

The Houston ask via bubs / d3 is the earliest pre-Austin Texas signal in the vault — a tiny but worth-noting datapoint on the multi-year drift south.

Open questions

  • Who are bubs and d3? (Bubs surfaces again in 2025 Bubba's note — likely dominic-orbit family-friend.)
  • Did Yahir actually go to Houston that summer, or did the trip get replaced by Vietnam?
  • Did the fixed-fee electricity musing ever resurface as a real product idea? (No trace yet.)

Wikilinks
